AWK.thesis: The thesis was an untouchable regulated monopoly: the largest private water utility in the US, 14 million customers, dividend growing ~8% annually for 18 consecutive years, and a mega-merger with Essential Utilities that would expand coverage to 17 states. Water = absolute necessity. No competition in any territory. Predictable revenue for decades.
AWK.whatHappened: 32 days after the pick, the position is essentially flat (-0.75%). There was no negative event — the business continues operating exactly as expected. What we underestimated was timing: utility stocks move with interest rate expectations. With rates staying high for longer, the market discounts future dividend streams less aggressively. The Essential Utilities merger is still in regulatory process (expected close Q1 2027).
AWK.lesson: Regulated utilities are defensive but not immune to the macro environment. A P/E of 22x in a high-rate environment means the market is already paying a premium for stability — and when rates don't fall, that premium doesn't expand. The long-term thesis remains intact, but we acknowledge we bought at a moment when the rate tailwind wasn't blowing in our favor.
